tasks.summary: IDispatcher interface shown in docs is simplified — missing many overloads and return type wrong
tasks.details: Docs show IDispatcher with 4 methods returning Task. Actual interface has 30+ overloads: (1) SendAsync returns IDeliveryReceipt not DeliveryReceipt, (2) LocalInvokeAsync returns ValueTask not Task, (3) Missing: void LocalInvokeAsync variants, object-based overloads, IMessageContext overloads with CallerMemberName/FilePath/LineNumber, DispatchOptions overloads, LocalInvokeWithReceiptAsync, LocalInvokeManyAsync. The docs Quick Reference table lists these batch methods but the interface code block doesn't show them.

tasks.summary: DeliveryReceipt shown as record(Guid, Guid, DateTimeOffset) — actual is a class with 7+ properties
tasks.details: Docs show: record DeliveryReceipt(Guid MessageId, Guid CorrelationId, DateTimeOffset Timestamp). Actual: sealed class implementing IDeliveryReceipt with MessageId (MessageId type, not Guid), CorrelationId (CorrelationId type), CausationId, Destination, Status (DeliveryStatus enum), StreamId, Metadata (IReadOnlyDictionary<string, JsonElement>), plus static factory methods (Accepted, Queued, Delivered, Failed). DeliveryStatus enum (Accepted/Queued/Delivered/Failed) not documented.

tasks.summary: LocalInvokeAndSyncAsync signature missing onWaiting/onDecisionMade callbacks
tasks.details: Docs show 3 params (message, timeout, ct). Actual has 5: message, timeout, onWaiting (Action<SyncWaitingContext>?), onDecisionMade (Action<SyncDecisionContext>?), ct. Also missing the TPerspective-specific overloads: LocalInvokeAndSyncAsync<TMessage, TResult, TPerspective> and LocalInvokeAndSyncForPerspectiveAsync<TMessage, TPerspective>.

tasks.summary: DispatchOptions class not fully documented
tasks.details: Docs mention DispatchOptions in the Quick Reference but don't document the class. Code has: CancellationToken, Timeout, WaitForPerspectives, PerspectiveWaitTimeout (default 30s), plus fluent methods WithCancellationToken(), WithTimeout(), WithPerspectiveWait(). Multiple dispatcher methods accept DispatchOptions. Should be a documented section.

tasks.summary: LocalInvokeWithReceiptAsync not documented
tasks.details: IDispatcher has LocalInvokeWithReceiptAsync<TMessage, TResult> returning InvokeResult<TResult> (which contains both Value and Receipt). This pattern is listed in the Quick Reference table but has no dedicated section explaining when/why to use it. InvokeResult<TResult> record also not documented.

tasks.summary: DispatchMode enum is [Flags] with composite values — docs don't explain this
tasks.details: DispatchMode is a [Flags] enum: None=0, LocalDispatch=1, Outbox=2, EventStore=4, Local=LocalDispatch|EventStore, LocalNoPersist=LocalDispatch, Both=LocalDispatch|Outbox, EventStoreOnly=EventStore. The composite nature (Local = LocalDispatch|EventStore) is important for understanding routing. Docs mention modes but don't explain the flags composition or all values (EventStoreOnly missing from docs).

tasks.summary: Route.EventStoreOnly() and Route.LocalNoPersist() not documented
tasks.details: Route static class has: None(), Local(), LocalNoPersist(), Outbox(), Both(), EventStoreOnly(). Docs only mention Local, Outbox, Both, None. Missing: EventStoreOnly (persist to event store without outbox publishing) and LocalNoPersist (local dispatch without event store persistence). Also missing: IEnumerable<> batch overloads for LocalNoPersist and EventStoreOnly.

tasks.summary: RebuildPerspectiveCommand shown with old 2-param signature (same as 2A-006)
tasks.details: routing.md line 143 shows RebuildPerspectiveCommand(string PerspectiveName, long? FromEventId). Actual code has 5 params: string[]? PerspectiveNames, RebuildMode Mode, Guid[]? IncludeStreamIds, Guid[]? ExcludeStreamIds, long? FromEventId. Also CancelPerspectiveRebuildCommand missing.

tasks.summary: PublishAsync return type wrong in docs — returns IDeliveryReceipt not void
tasks.details: Docs say Pattern 3 PublishAsync returns 'Task' (void). Quick Reference table says 'void'. Actual signature: Task<IDeliveryReceipt> PublishAsync<TEvent>(TEvent eventData). It returns a delivery receipt, not void. This affects how users understand event publishing.

tasks.summary: ILifecycleContext missing AttemptNumber and ProcessingMode properties in docs
tasks.details: ILifecycleContext in code has: CurrentStage, EventId, StreamId, PerspectiveType, LastProcessedEventId, MessageSource, AttemptNumber (int?), ProcessingMode (ProcessingMode?). The docs reference ILifecycleContext but don't show the full interface. AttemptNumber enables retry-aware logic. ProcessingMode (Live/Replay/Rebuild) enables mode-aware behavior.

tasks.summary: ProcessingMode enum not documented
tasks.details: ProcessingMode enum (Live=0, Replay=1, Rebuild=2) is available via ILifecycleContext.ProcessingMode but not documented. Users writing lifecycle receptors need to know about this for replay-safe logic (e.g., don't send emails during Replay/Rebuild).

tasks.summary: IReceptorRegistry interface differs from docs — Register/Unregister have IMessage constraint
tasks.details: Docs show ILifecycleReceptorRegistry for runtime registration. Actual IReceptorRegistry has Register<TMessage>(receptor, stage) and Unregister<TMessage>(receptor, stage) with 'where TMessage : IMessage' constraint. Need to verify which interface users actually interact with and ensure docs match.

tasks.summary: IReceptor<TMessage> (void variant) interface not shown in docs
tasks.details: Docs show IReceptor<TMessage, TResponse> but the void variant IReceptor<TMessage> (ValueTask HandleAsync, no return) is not shown in the interface section. It's used extensively in lifecycle receptors. The code has both interfaces in IReceptor.cs.

tasks.summary: IReceptor generic constraint changed — docs show 'where TMessage : notnull' but verify actual
tasks.details: Docs IReceptor shows 'where TMessage : notnull'. IReceptorRegistry.Register has 'where TMessage : IMessage'. Need to verify if IReceptor itself has a different constraint in the actual code, and document consistently.

tasks.summary: CascadeMessageAsync not documented
tasks.details: IDispatcher has CascadeMessageAsync(IMessage, IMessageEnvelope?, DispatchMode, CancellationToken) for manual cascade control. Not mentioned in dispatcher docs. May be an advanced/internal API but should at least be mentioned.

tasks.summary: Tags should include more synonyms for search
tasks.details: Current tags: 'dispatcher, messaging, commands, events, patterns'. Should add: 'SendAsync, LocalInvokeAsync, PublishAsync, CQRS, command handler, message bus, mediator, in-process RPC, delivery receipt, event broadcasting, batch dispatch, SendManyAsync'.

tasks.summary: Page missing frontmatter fields
tasks.details: routing.md appears to have no frontmatter at all (starts with '# Namespace-Based Routing'). Needs: title, version, category, order, description, tags, codeReferences. Tags should include: 'routing, namespace, topics, inbox, outbox, subscription, command routing, event routing, SharedTopicInboxStrategy, DomainTopicInboxStrategy, OwnDomains, SubscribeTo'.

tasks.summary: Tags should include more synonyms
tasks.details: Current tags: 'lifecycle, receptors, FireAt, attributes, ILifecycleContext, AOT, source-generators'. Should add: 'hooks, middleware, cross-cutting concerns, test synchronization, PostPerspectiveInline, scoped dependencies, replay safety, FireDuringReplay, processing mode'.

tasks.summary: All code samples have auto-generated poor metadata throughout
tasks.details: All ~20 code samples in dispatcher.md have description fields that just say 'Demonstrates X' where X is the title. Tags are meaningless word splits from titles (e.g., ['Fundamentals', 'Dispatcher', 'Basic', 'Usage']). Need meaningful descriptions explaining what each sample demonstrates, proper categorized tags, and testFile/testMethod links where applicable.

tasks.summary: All code samples have auto-generated poor metadata
tasks.details: Same issue as dispatcher.md — all ~15 code samples have auto-generated descriptions and garbage tags. Need review for meaningful metadata.

tasks.summary: Code sample metadata has callout syntax leaking into description
tasks.details: lifecycle-receptors.md line 64: code sample has description=':::new For receptors that perform pure computation...' — the callout syntax leaked into the metadata. Line 207 has description with table markdown. Multiple samples throughout have this issue. All ~10 code samples need metadata cleanup.

tasks.summary: Async receptor example uses Task.Delay(1) as placeholder — poor pattern
tasks.details: dispatcher.md line 338: AsyncOrderReceptor example has 'await Task.Delay(1)' as a placeholder for async work. Should use a meaningful async operation (like DbContext.SaveChangesAsync) or remove the await entirely if demonstrating the pattern.

tasks.summary: No mermaid diagrams — ASCII flow diagrams could be mermaid for RTD compatibility
tasks.details: dispatcher.md uses ASCII art for flows (SendAsync Flow, LocalInvokeAsync Flow). These should be converted to mermaid sequence/flow diagrams for better rendering on both Angular site and RTD. routing.md also has ASCII flows.

tasks.summary: System commands duplicated in routing.md and lifecycle.md
tasks.details: routing.md lines 136-169 show full System Commands code block (RebuildPerspectiveCommand, ClearCacheCommand, DiagnosticsCommand, PauseProcessingCommand, ResumeProcessingCommand) — same content as lifecycle.md. Should reference lifecycle.md or a dedicated system commands page instead of duplicating.

tasks.summary: codeReferences includes sample file that may not exist
tasks.details: dispatcher.md codeReferences includes 'samples/ECommerce/ECommerce.BFF.API/Endpoints/OrderEndpoints.cs'. Need to verify this sample file exists in the library repo. If samples have been reorganized, the reference may be broken.

tasks.summary: No contributor docs for receptor invocation internals
tasks.details: The extending/ section should have contributor-facing docs explaining how ReceptorInvoker works internally, how the source generator creates LifecycleInvoker code, and how the scoped dependency injection is generated. Would help contributors understand the generated code patterns.

sectionSummary: The dispatcher and receptors sections are the most important user-facing docs. The biggest issues are: (1) IDispatcher interface shown in docs is heavily simplified — missing 25+ overloads, wrong return types (Task vs ValueTask, DeliveryReceipt vs IDeliveryReceipt), (2) DeliveryReceipt structure is completely wrong (simple record vs full class with Status, Metadata, etc.), (3) Several dispatch modes and route methods not documented (EventStoreOnly, LocalNoPersist, DispatchOptions), (4) LocalInvokeAndSyncAsync missing callback params and TPerspective overloads, (5) SystemCommands signature drift (same as 2A), (6) Code sample metadata is universally poor across all 3 pages. The conceptual documentation is strong — the routing page explains namespace-based routing well, and the lifecycle receptors page has good coverage of FireAt and scoped dependencies. The main gaps are in API accuracy.
